title: Containerfile parameter expansion doesn't correctly distinguish unset from empty
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

In response to #4284, parameter expansion hasn't completely correctly implemented.

A feature of Dockerfile syntax (1.6.0) is [shell parameter expansion](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4284). In Docker Engine, Podman and nerdctl, these can be used to, e.g., fail a build when a build arg is unset (`{VAR?}` or when empty or unset `{VAR:?}`). I haven't found documentation for the feature by Docker Inc. The semantics differ between Docker Engine, on the one hand, and Podman and nerdctl on the other.

```Dockerfile
# syntax=docker.io/docker/dockerfile:1@sha256:ac85f380a63b13dfcefa89046420e1781752bab202122f8f50032edf31be0021
ARG IMAGE_PREFIX=
ARG DISTRO=alpine
ARG VERSION_DISTRO=3.19
FROM ${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman build .`


**Describe the results you received:**

Build succeeds.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Build fails, with a fault similar to with `docker buildx`'s:

```text
ERROR: failed to solve: failed to process "${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}": IMAGE_PREFIX: is not allowed to be empty
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

comments.body: Interested in working on a fix?

I don't this this bug is present on podman and buildah upstream.

```console
fl@fl-pc:~/work/buildah/bin$ cat Dockerfile 
ARG IMAGE_PREFIX=
ARG DISTRO=alpine
ARG VERSION_DISTRO=3.19
FROM ${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}
RUN echo hello
fl@fl-pc:~/work/buildah/bin$ ./buildah build -t test .
Error: while replacing arg variables with values for format "${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}": Failed to process `${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}`: IMAGE_PREFIX is not allowed to be unset
fl@fl-pc:~/work/buildah/bin$ ^C

```

and podman
```console
fl@fl-pc:~/work/podman/bin$ cat Dockerfile 
ARG IMAGE_PREFIX=
ARG DISTRO=alpine
ARG VERSION_DISTRO=3.19
FROM ${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}
RUN echo hello
fl@fl-pc:~/work/podman/bin$ ./podman build -t test .
Error: while replacing arg variables with values for format "${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}": Failed to process `${IMAGE_PREFIX:?}${DISTRO:?}:${VERSION_DISTRO:?}`: IMAGE_PREFIX is not allowed to be unset
fl@fl-pc:~/work/podman/bin$ 


Please try using upstream version, I'm closing this issue but please feel free to re-open this.
